articles.title: Uncle Sam is wrong, India and China are doing their bit to fight climate change
articles.summary: It is true that there is an increase in carbon emission by India and China due to homegrown consumption and lack of infrastructure. But let s keep in mind that India s carbon emissions per capita are still 10 times lower and China s is half of that compared to the US.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Multibagger defence stock Apollo Micro Systems close to record high. Do you own?
articles.summary: Apollo Micro Systems rose over 2% on 9 June after announcing the trading approval of 13,000 newly allotted shares. The company also reported strong Q4FY26 earnings, with net profit of ₹38 crore and year-on-year revenue growth of 81%.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Vodafone Idea share price jumps over 3% after Bombay High Court relief on spectrum charges
articles.summary: Vodafone Idea share price has gained 30% in one month, and has rallied 47% in three months. The telecom stock has surged 36% in six months, while it has jumped 106% in one year.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Small-cap stock BlueStone Jewellery share price falls 6.5% as investors offload stake worth ₹300 crore via block deal
articles.summary: BlueStone Jewellery share price falls 6.5% asinvestors sell stake via block deal. The floor price for the transaction was fixed at ₹520 per share, implying a discount of around 5.4% to Bluestone's closing price of ₹549.50 on the NSE on June 8, 2026.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: RVNL share price gains over 3% after order win, breaks 9-day losing run
articles.summary: RVNL share price has fallen over 23% in one month, and has declined 15% in three months. The railway PSU stock has dropped 35% on a year-to-date (YTD) basis, while it has plunged 45% in one year.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Rupee appreciates 24 paise to open at 95.47 against US dollar
articles.summary: The Indian rupee rose 24 paise to 95.47 against the US dollar, aided by falling crude prices after Iran and Israel paused hostilities. The RBI's new measures aim to boost foreign currency inflows and stabilize the rupee, which has weakened nearly 8% this year.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Silver price today down 1% to ₹2.43 lakh as bond yields rise, Fed rate hike fears return despite Israel-Iran ceasefire
articles.summary: Silver prices fell to ₹2.43 lakh per kg as rising US Treasury yields and Federal Reserve rate hike expectations overshadowed easing geopolitical tensions. Investors remain cautious ahead of US inflation data, which may influence silver's near-term direction.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Zepto IPO: Quick commerce firm files updated DRHP, aims to raise ₹8,010 crore
articles.summary: Zepto has filed an updated draft red herring prospectus with SEBI for its IPO, including a fresh share issue of ₹8,010 crore and an OFS of 113 million shares. The company aims for a July stock market debut, following approval from SEBI in May.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Gold rate today: MCX gold price falls below ₹1.55 lakh per 10 grams, silver rate slips 1%; What’s the strategy ahead?
articles.summary: Gold rate today: Gold prices steadied in the international market amid a fragile ceasefire between Israel and Iran, while investors await signs of progress in the broader Middle East conflict, with concerns over inflation and interest rate hikes in focus.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: NLC India OFS: Navratna PSU stock falls 5% as govt to sell 3% stake - Check floor price, key dates, and other details
articles.summary: The government has initiated an Offer for Sale (OFS) of up to 3% in NLC India Ltd, including a green shoe option. The floor price is set at ₹303 per share. This follows successful stake sales in other companies and is part of the ongoing disinvestment program in FY27.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Is the stock market close to a bottom? Blip in global AI play along with bearish bets suggest so
articles.summary: Analysts see signs of a market bottom as FPI long-short ratio falls to a deeply oversold 7.58%, with bearish futures positions hovering near record highs seen in March. A global anti-AI trade for now and possible peace prospects in West Asia could be the silver linings for India.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Stock market today: Gift Nifty hints muted opening; US-Iran war, gold to crude oil prices — eight stocks to buy or sell
articles.summary: Indian stock markets are expected to open flat-to-negative on June 9, 2026, with Gift Nifty at 23,121.5. This follows a significant drop on Monday due to geopolitical tensions and rising crude prices, amid cautious global sentiment and increasing market volatility.

articles.summary: Nifty 50, Sensex prediction today: The trends on Gift Nifty also indicate a mildly negative start for the Indian benchmark index. The Gift Nifty was trading around 23,137 level, a discount of nearly 38 points from the Nifty futures’ previous close.

articles.title: Rail Vikas Nigam shares to be in focus on Tuesday as company secures ₹221 crore order
articles.summary: Rail Vikas Nigam secured a ₹221.33 crore EPC contract from South East Central Railway for interlocking and signalling works across multiple stations. Despite recent order wins, the stock has faced selling pressure due to weak earnings, resulting in a year-to-date loss of 36%.

articles.title: Marvell Technology shares jump over 15% on S&P 500 entry; up 250% in 2026
articles.summary: On 8 June, Marvell Technology's stock rose 15.7% to $305 after the announcement of its inclusion in the S&P 500 on 22 June. This marks a recovery driven by a GAAP profit and highlights the growing influence of AI on semiconductor demand and stock performance.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Govt to sell up to 3% stake in NLC India via OFS at ₹303 per share
articles.summary: The government could raise about ₹840 crore through the base offer of 2%. If the additional 1% greenshoe option is fully exercised, the proceeds could rise to around ₹1,260 crore.

articles.title: Gold, silver rates today: Comex gold, silver rebound from lows as Iran ceasefire hopes lift sentiment
articles.summary: Precious metals recovered some losses amid improving ceasefire prospects between Israel and Iran, but remain pressured by strong US jobs data suggesting a potential interest rate hike.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: West Asia war: Trump says Iran, Israel seek immediate ceasefire. What does it mean for Indian stock market?
articles.summary: The Indian stock market anticipates a rebound as Middle Eastern tensions ease with Iran's military halting operations. Investors are focused on crude oil price fluctuations, which could significantly impact market dynamics, especially in light of recent statements from US officials.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Wall Street climbs on signs of easing tensions in Middle East, chip stocks rally
articles.summary: As of 10:30 a.m. Eastern Time, the S&P 500 rose 0.8%, the Dow Jones Industrial Average was up 0.3%, and the Nasdaq Composite was 1.3% higher.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: US stock market today: Nasdaq, S&P 500 futures rebound as chip stocks recover; Marvell Tech jumps 8%
articles.summary: US stock futures rose on 8 June, driven by a rebound in chip stocks despite rising oil prices and bond yields amid escalating Middle East tensions. S&P 500 futures increased by 0.3%, while Nasdaq 100 futures climbed 0.7%, signalling a pause in the technology-led sell-off.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: India's growth silver linings face war and drought clouds
articles.summary: India’s economy ended FY26 with a strong 7.7% GDP growth driven by healthy agricultural activity and likely private investments, but FY27 faces risks on all these fronts from inflation and a below-normal monsoon.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: Oil prices ease after Israel, Iran halt attacks; markets eye ceasefire
articles.summary: Brent and WTI edged lower after Israel and Iran paused attacks, easing immediate supply concerns and offering potential relief to oil-importing economies such as India.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: Solar sector feels the heat as cost spike threatens project economics
articles.summary: A surge in the prices of metals and petrochemical inputs linked to the West Asia war has pushed up costs for India's solar power sector by around 20%. The rise, including a 30-35% increase in solar cell prices, could hit developers’ returns and increase their financing costs for projects.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Focus on domestic economy as India buffeted by headwinds, says senior official
articles.summary: Despite rising crude oil prices due to the Iran war, India remains focused on domestic growth, says a senior government functionary. GST collections show strength, and recent RBI reforms aim to enhance foreign investment in government securities.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Why India Inc's rising profit share in GDP will be tough to sustain in FY27
articles.summary: Driven by rising earnings, the profit-to-GDP ratio for the BSE 500 universe reached 4.3% in FY26. However, looming global tensions and softening consumption indicators threaten to halt further expansion of this corporate profit share.
articles.label: BULLISH
